item.title: India Raises Diesel, Gasoline Prices for Third Time in Eight Days
item.description: India’s state-run refiners raised retail prices again of diesel and gasoline on Saturday to help processors cut losses on discounted sales and to control a spike in demand.

item.title: Abrego Garcia Wins Dismissal of US Human-Smuggling Case
item.description: A federal judge in Tennessee dismissed human-smuggling charges against Salvadoran migrant Kilmar Armando Abrego Garcia after concluding the US government brought the case in retaliation for his legal fight against being deported.

item.title: Zoom’s Anthropic Investment Has Netted the Company $1 Billion
item.description: Zoom Communications Inc., the videoconferencing company, has netted about $1 billion on an investment it made in artificial intelligence startup Anthropic PBC in early 2023.

item.title: Equatorial Leans Against Bidding on Copasa Privatization
item.description: Equatorial SA is unlikely to bid on a key stake in Copasa, the Brazilian water utility that’s being privatized by the government of Minas Gerais state, according to people familiar to the matter.

item.title: US Weighs Chip Tariffs to Spur Domestic Growth, Trade Chief Says
item.description: The Trump administration continues to weigh US tariffs on imported semiconductors to boost domestic chip manufacturing, though there are no immediate plans to impose any new levies, US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer said.

item.title: Bezos’ Blue Origin Plans $600 Million Expansion in Florida
item.description: Blue Origin, the aerospace company founded by billionaire Jeff Bezos, will invest $600 million to expand its Rocket Park campus in Cape Canaveral, Florida, according to a statement from Governor Ron DeSantis.

item.title: Moody’s Lifts South Africa Outlook to Postive on Fiscal Boost
item.description: Moody’s Ratings revised South Africa’s credit outlook to positive, citing the country’s improving fiscal position and commitment to reforms.

item.title: Nasdaq Gets Approval From SEC to List Bitcoin Index Options
item.description: The Securities and Exchange Commission has given the go-ahead for Nasdaq Inc. to list index options based on the price of Bitcoin, the latest sign that Wall Street is becoming more tightly integrated with the world of digital assets.

item.title: AI’s Expanding Market Grip Traps Active Managers on Wall Street
item.description: The AI boom is humbling the humans trying to navigate the markets it increasingly distorts.

item.title: Lantheus Explores Potential $7 Billion Sale Following Offer From Curium
item.description: Lantheus Holdings Inc. is weighing a sale after getting a takeover offer from Curium that values the radiopharma company at about $7 billion, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Anthropic to Close Over $30 Billion Round as Soon as Next Week
item.description: Anthropic PBC is set to close its latest round of funding, which may top $30 billion at a valuation above $900 billion, as soon as next week, according to people familiar with the matter, vaulting ahead of rival OpenAI to become the world’s most valuable AI startup.

item.title: Danaher Raises $3 Billion in Record Private-Placement Bond Sale
item.description: Danaher Corp. has completed what is being called a record $3 billion private-placement bond sale.

item.title: Paraguay Central Bank Keeps 5.5% Rate, Says Policy Neutral
item.description: Paraguay’s central bank kept its key interest rate unchanged for a third-consecutive month at 5.5%, saying the level was consistent with a neutral monetary policy stance.

item.title: SEC Delays Plan Allowing for Crypto Versions of US Stocks
item.description: The Securities and Exchange Commission is delaying a plan to provide broad exemptions for US crypto firms to trade tokenized assets linked to stocks, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: US Oil Rig Count Up by Most in Four Years Amid Drilling Recovery
item.description: The number of rigs drilling in the US shale patch rose by the most in more than four years, the latest sign of a resurgence in domestic activity as the war in Iran drives up oil prices.

item.title: Dollar Stalls as Hope for US-Iran Peace Boosts Risk Sentiment
item.description: The dollar is set to end the week nearly unchanged as risk assets got a boost tied to optimism around progress in peace talks between the US and Iran.

item.title: Debt Investors Press Quebec Finance Minister on Separatism in Canada
item.description: Canada is dealing with active separatist movements in two of its largest provinces, sparking wariness among bondholders in the US, according to Quebec’s finance minister.

item.title: SEC Delays Prediction Market ETFs From Roundhill, GraniteShares and Bitwise
item.description: Upstart fund issuers raced to wrap election and recession bets into tickers. Then the regulator stepped in.

item.title: ‘Disco Elysium’ Maker Releases New Game Under a Shadow of Controversy
item.description: Five years after losing many of its top leaders, the critically acclaimed ZA/UM is back with ‘Zero Parades’

item.title: Futu, Up Fintech Options Surged Before Crackdown Sparked Slump
item.description: Trading in US-listed options on Futu Holdings Ltd. and Up Fintech Holding Ltd. surged just before China’s regulator’s announcement that it planned to penalize them triggered a record share slump.

item.title: Trafigura Withdraws Huge Volumes of Copper From LME Warehouses
item.description: Trafigura Group has moved to withdraw hundreds of millions of dollars of copper from London Metal Exchange warehouses as lucrative trading opportunities emerge in the US and China, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Uber Said to Study Full Takeover of Germany’s Delivery Hero
item.description: Uber Technologies Inc. is exploring options for a full takeover of Delivery Hero SE, people familiar with the matter said, a move that would help it better compete with DoorDash Inc. outside the US.

item.title: Central Africa States Rule Out CFA Franc Devaluation, Governor Says
item.description: Central African countries have no plans to devalue their common currency despite economic pressure caused by weak growth and low foreign-exchange reserves, according to Bank of Central African States Governor Yvon Sana Bangui.

item.title: Bill Winters ‘Lower-Value Human’ Apology Not Enough for Unions
item.description: Bill Winters’ efforts to restore calm after making some controversial comments on AI have failed to reassure labor organizations, according to one of the world’s largest union federations.

item.title: Musk Could Earn $760 Billion From SpaceX Mars-Shot Pay Packages
item.description: Elon Musk is making a habit of collecting 12-figure compensation packages.

item.title: Food and Fuel Run Low as Blockades Cripple Bolivian Economy
item.description: More than three weeks of anti-government blockades are choking off food, fuel and medicine supply in Bolivia’s administrative capital La Paz, threatening to bring economic activity to a halt.

item.title: Big Euro-Zone Economies Are Enduring Unfolding Inflation Shock
item.description: Inflation in the euro zone’s four biggest economies probably either jumped in May or held to an already robust pace, fueling the case for an interest-rate hike.

item.title: Argentina in Talks to Extend Repo Maturities Beyond Election
item.description: Argentine policymakers are negotiating with major international banks to extend maturities on repurchase agreements — or repos — to ease the government debt burden going into the 2027 election year.

item.title: ECB’s Muller Sees ‘Good Case’ for June Hike on Energy Surge
item.description: There’s a solid argument for the European Central Bank to lift interest rates next month in response to the Iran war, according to outgoing Governing Council member Madis Muller.

item.title: What the 2023 Bank Runs Can Tell Us About Private Credit Stress
item.description: How the SVB run explains the true nature of the private credit market.
